open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own browser tab). viewer.js loadDocument() (the single LOAD boundary) still has no numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and no processing timeout: after getDocument(...).promise it emits PDF_LOADED + calls renderAll() (TASK-316 supersede-race guard present + re-verified this run at lines 123/129/171/189, so footprint is linear — one canvas per page — not multiplied). All per-page / whole-doc consumers sit downstream of this one uncapped boundary and are NOT new load boundaries: (1) viewer.js renderAll() one canvas/page (dpr capped at 2); (2) thumbnails.js a 2nd canvas/page (mitigated by 120px width + renderToken guard); (3) merge.js mergeAndDownload() loads base+each queued file with no cumulative byte/page cap; (4) pages.js/page-numbers.js/metadata.js getData()->pdf-lib.load()->save() over the open doc; (5) convert.js renders ONE page to a throwaway canvas with its own MAX=8000px cap; (6) NEW this tick: bates.js (TASK-333) getData()->pdf-lib.load()->drawText per page->save() over the already-open doc — same class, operates only on the validated open document, needs no separate cap. Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects every downstream consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

audit_notes.bates_js: TASK-333 stamp legal Bates IDs onto every page. Operates on the already-open doc via currentDoc.getData() -> pdf-lib load/drawText/save -> Blob download (NOT a new load boundary). XSS-safe: prefix/suffix sanitized to WinAnsi-printable ASCII (0x20-0x7e, 0xa1-0xff) and capped at 32 chars (sanitizeAffix) so Helvetica embedder cannot throw; user text reaches ONLY pdf-lib drawText and statusEl.textContent, never innerHTML. Numeric controls clamped (start 0..1e8, digits 1..12). buildFileName() strips to [a-zA-Z0-9._-], slice(0,180), default 'document' -> <base>_bates.pdf. Safe blob+createObjectURL+a.download+rel=noopener, anchor removed, URL revoked after 1s. Controls disabled until PDF_LOADED; no-doc Apply shows 'Load a PDF first.' status, never throws; errors caught + surfaced via EventBus.ERROR.
audit_notes.zoom_menu_js: TASK-332 accessible zoom-preset dropdown on #zoom-level. 100% static developer strings; listEl cleared via textContent='', menuitems via btn.textContent (never innerHTML). No user/PDF content, no external requests, no eval. Talks only to viewer.js public API (setScale) + ActionRegistry + EventBus. setOpen() guards against opening while disabled/no-doc; activate() wraps action in try/catch so a selection can never surface an exception. No layout/render-core changes.
audit_notes.viewer_js_changes: Added rotation-aware fitPage(), actualSize()(=setScale(1.0)), and a shared page1Viewport() helper reused by fitWidth()/fitPage() (no duplicated measurement math). Pure zoom/layout math + no-doc guards; renderAll() core and the TASK-316 supersede guards are untouched. loadDocument() STILL has no numPages cap -> SEC-002 unchanged/open (see open_known_gaps). No new load boundary, no new security surface.
audit_notes.innerHTML_in_js: only empty-string clears or comments across the whole js/ tree — no user/PDF content sink anywhere
audit_notes.external_fetch_or_xhr: none — no fetch/XMLHttpRequest/sendBeacon/WebSocket and no non-local http(s) URLs in js/ (100% client-side, PDFs never leave the browser)
audit_notes.secrets_scan: git grep for private keys / AWS keys / sk- tokens / inline passwords over tracked tree (excluding *.md and status/*.json) -> no real secret
